• We want a prior that (a) respects positivity, (b) allows right‐skew, and (c) encodes moderate uncertainty (spread of about a factor of ~1.3–1.4 between lower and upper 95% bounds). A lognormal prior on the true mean fits well.

– exp(11.98) = 160 000 (median)

– Mean(real) = exp(μ + σ²/2) = exp(11.98 + 0.01125) ≈ exp(11.99125) ≈ \$162 000.
